defamation.facts: Newspaper published false allegations about business practices causing reputation damage
defamation.holding: False statements published with negligence constitute defamation. Damages awarded.
defamation.judgment: The publication of defamatory material in newspaper with proof of actual malice entitles plaintiff to damages. Court awarded Rs. 5,00,000 as compensation.
defamation.principle: Truth, public interest, and fair comment are defenses to defamation

defamation.case_name: Meera Kapoor v. Social Media Platform
defamation.facts: False allegations posted on social media platform damaging reputation
defamation.holding: Social media platforms can be held liable for defamatory content if they do not remove after notice
defamation.applicable_law: IPC Sections 499-500 with IT Act provisions
defamation.judgment: Platform directed to remove defamatory posts within 24 hours of notice. Damages awarded.
defamation.principle: Internet service providers have responsibility to monitor defamatory content

labour_employment.facts: Workers terminated without notice or compensation after 15 years of service
labour_employment.holding: Unjustified termination without following proper procedure violates labor laws
labour_employment.applicable_law: Industrial Disputes Act 1947, Code of Wages 2019
labour_employment.judgment: Workers entitled to reinstatement with back wages and compensation. Rs. 50 lakhs awarded.
labour_employment.principle: Employers must follow due process for termination and provide notice period

labour_employment.facts: Employee worked 60 hours per week without overtime compensation for 2 years
labour_employment.holding: Overtime compensation is mandatory for hours beyond standard working hours
labour_employment.judgment: Employer ordered to pay overtime compensation at 1.5x rate for all additional hours. Rs. 8 lakhs awarded.
labour_employment.principle: Overtime compensation is a statutory right not subject to waiver

property_law.facts: Property transfer disputed due to incomplete documentation and lack of consideration
property_law.holding: Valid transfer requires proper documentation and sufficient consideration
property_law.applicable_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882, Indian Succession Act 1925
property_law.judgment: Transfer declared void due to lack of consideration. Property reverted to original owner.
property_law.principle: Every property transfer must comply with statutory formalities

property_law.facts: Landlord unlawfully increased rent by 200% and threatened eviction
property_law.holding: Rent increase must follow statutory procedure with notice and justification
property_law.applicable_law: Property laws with rent control provisions
property_law.judgment: Rent increase limited to 10% annually. Landlord directed to provide 3 months notice.
property_law.principle: Tenant rights are protected against arbitrary eviction and excessive rent hikes

cyber_law.facts: Unauthorized access to bank servers resulted in data breach affecting 50,000 customers
cyber_law.holding: Hacking with intent to cause damage is a serious cybercrime with significant penalties
cyber_law.judgment: Convicted hackers sentenced to 3 years imprisonment and Rs. 5 lakh fine each.
cyber_law.principle: Cybersecurity is critical infrastructure; unauthorized access is severely punished

family_law.facts: Wife filed for divorce citing cruelty and dowry demands
family_law.holding: Excessive dowry demands and mental cruelty are valid grounds for divorce
family_law.judgment: Divorce granted. Husband ordered to pay maintenance Rs. 25,000 monthly. Dowry amount refunded.
family_law.principle: Dowry is illegal; its demand constitutes cruelty under family law

family_law.facts: Groom and family demanded dowry of Rs. 10 lakhs and jewelry
family_law.holding: Dowry demand is criminal offense under Dowry Prohibition Act
family_law.judgment: All defendants sentenced to 2 years imprisonment and fine of Rs. 1 lakh each.
family_law.principle: Dowry is illegal; both demanding and giving dowry are criminal acts

criminal_law.facts: Death occurred due to rash and negligent driving resulting in vehicle collision
criminal_law.holding: Rash and negligent driving causing death is culpable homicide not amounting to murder
criminal_law.judgment: Convicted. Sentenced to 5 years imprisonment and disqualified from driving for life.
criminal_law.principle: Negligent acts causing death are seriously punished under criminal law

contract_law.facts: Service provider failed to deliver contracted services within agreed timeline
contract_law.holding: Breach of contract entitles non-breaching party to compensation
contract_law.judgment: Damages awarded at Rs. 20 lakhs for lost profits and business disruption.
contract_law.principle: Contracts are enforceable; breach results in damage compensation

consumer_law.facts: Mobile phone battery exploded within warranty period causing injury
consumer_law.holding: Manufacturers are liable for defective products causing injury
consumer_law.judgment: Manufacturer ordered to pay Rs. 2 lakhs compensation for injury and loss.
consumer_law.principle: Consumer safety is paramount; manufacturers have strict liability
